title: AHOL Tier 3 Task Runner Return
description: Schema for the JSON object returned by an AHOL Tier 3 task-runner to its Tier 2 variant-runner parent. Each task-runner executes exactly one benchmark task inside an isolated container, writes a row to the benchmarks SQLite database, and returns this compact result. Prose returns are a bug; the runner must validate this dict before exit.

task_id.description: Stable identifier of the benchmark task that was executed (for example swe-bench-lite-astropy-12345 or ahol-internal-fizzbuzz-01). Must be unique within a benchmark suite.

passed.description: True if the task's built-in verifier returned success. False for test failures, timeouts, or crashes. Infrastructure failures that prevent producing a verdict should set passed=false AND populate error_summary.

tokens_used.description: Total tokens consumed by the inner model call or agent loop for this single task. Hard-capped at 2,000,000 to catch runaway loops before they skew the variant total.

wall_clock_sec.description: Wall-clock seconds from task-runner entry to return. Includes container spin-up only if measured inside the runner.

patch_sha.description: Either the 40-character lowercase hex SHA-1 of the patch the variant produced, OR the literal sentinel string 'no_patch' when the variant chose not to emit a patch, OR null when the runner crashed before it could hash a patch. Verified by the variant-runner against the container artifact store.

error_summary.description: Short human-readable summary of any error encountered (verifier exception, container OOM, model API error). Null on a clean pass or a clean test failure. Used by the variant-runner to distinguish task-level failures from infrastructure problems.
